destinations.evidence: Long-running HTTP payload validation decodes the executor request fields, enforces base executor/TTL checks, validates URLs, and validates the Phase 2 deadline offset.
RPC admission invokes metadata-routed payload validation for long-running HTTP detected calls.

equivalence_argument: The port uses a specific LongRunningHTTPCallRequest decoder but still applies the common executor-request validation semantics required for executor, TTL, secrets/key, URL, and Phase 2 bounds.
rationale: The commit body explains that LongRunningHTTPCallRequest has additional ABI fields and was failing RPC validation when decoded as HTTPCallRequest; the diff splits LONG_RUNNING_HTTP_PRECOMPILE onto validate_executor_request. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: When validate_async_payload receives LONG_RUNNING_HTTP_PRECOMPILE input, it must validate the base executor request fields rather than attempting to decode the payload as a regular HTTPCallRequest.
suggested_falsification_tests: Submit a LONG_RUNNING_HTTP_PRECOMPILE payload with valid executor fields and long-running-only ABI fields that would fail HTTPCallRequest decoding; validate_async_payload should accept it, while malformed executor address or TTL should still be rejected.
summary: Long-running HTTP RPC validation uses executor-request checks.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Submit long-running HTTP payloads with malformed ABI, zero executor, invalid TTL, bad URL, and invalid max_poll_block; RPC should reject each.
title: Long-running HTTP uses executor-request validation
